---
title: "FHIR: Inter-Hospital Interoperability"
lang: en-GB
---

## Introduction {#sec-09en-intro .unnumbered}

The previous chapter built the `FHIR` vocabulary: what a `Resource` is, how
identifiers work, what primitive and composite types are, what a
`CodeableConcept` is, and how a `Bundle` acts as an envelope. That vocabulary
is necessary ÔÇö but not sufficient. Knowing what a `Patient` or an
`Observation` is does not explain *how* those resources connect to model a
real clinical workflow, nor how that connection supports the exchange of
information between different institutions.

This chapter uses the vocabulary to build workflows. The focus shifts from
the individual structure of each resource to the **network of relationships**
that links them: how an `Encounter` articulates `Patient`, `Practitioner` and
`Organization`; how a `DiagnosticReport` aggregates `Observation`; how an
`ImagingStudy` references `DICOM` images without duplicating them. And,
finally, how that graph of resources can be transported between different
servers ÔÇö with its guarantees and its real limitations.

## From Structure to Flow {#sec-09en-estrutura-fluxo}

The starting point is a transition table: what was covered in the previous
chapter and what this chapter adds to each concept.

| Concept (Lecture 8) | What we learned | What this lecture adds |
|:--------------------|:----------------|:-----------------------|
| `Resource` | Atomic unit of clinical information with `id` and `meta` | How it connects to other resources ÔÇö via `Reference()` |
| `Identifier` | Logical id vs business identifier (`MRN`, `NIF`, `NHC`) | The same patient has different IDs in each system |
| `CodeableConcept` | Code + terminology system (`SNOMED`, `LOINC`) | Codes must be compatible to integrate observations |
| `Bundle` | Envelope of multiple resources in a single response | Types `document`, `transaction`, `searchset`, `batch` |

: Transition from Lecture 8 to Lecture 9 ÔÇö from structure to flow. {#tbl-09en-transicao}

The central idea of this chapter can be summarised in one sentence:
interoperability is not merely message exchange ÔÇö it is a **graph of
identified, referenced and standardised clinical entities**. The same patient
exists in multiple systems; `FHIR` does not duplicate the data, it links them.

## `Reference()` ÔÇö How Resources Connect {#sec-09en-reference}

### Anatomy of a `Reference()` {#sec-09en-reference-anatomy}

A `Reference()` is a typed pointer to another resource ÔÇö local, on another
server, or embedded within the resource itself. It does not copy the data; it
points to it. The client that needs the data resolves the reference with a
`GET` to the target `URL`.

A `Reference()` has three fields [@HL7_FHIR_R4]:

| Field | Role | Example |
|:------|:-----|:--------|
| `reference` | `URL` of the target resource ÔÇö relative or absolute | `"Patient/123"` or `"https://fhir.snsh.pt/Patient/456"` |
| `type` | Expected resource type ÔÇö optional but recommended | `"Patient"`, `"Practitioner"`, `"Organization"` |
| `display` | Human-readable text ÔÇö not the canonical data | `"Ana Ferreira"` ÔÇö does not replace the name in the `Patient` resource |

: Fields of a FHIR R4 Reference() [@HL7_FHIR_R4]. {#tbl-09en-reference-fields}

The `display` field is for presentation only ÔÇö if the system updates the
patient's name in the `Patient` resource, the `display` field in any
`Reference()` pointing to that patient is not updated automatically. The
canonical data always resides in the referenced resource, not in the reference.

```json
{
  "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
  "id":           "report-987",
  "status":       "final",
  "code":         { "text": "Full blood count" },
  "subject": {
    "reference": "Patient/123",
    "type":      "Patient",
    "display":   "Ana Ferreira"
  },
  "performer": [{
    "reference": "Practitioner/dr-costa",
    "type":      "Practitioner",
    "display":   "Dr. Lu├¡s Costa"
  }],
  "result": [
    { "reference": "Observation/obs-1" },
    { "reference": "Observation/obs-2" }
  ]
}
```

In this `DiagnosticReport`, the `subject` field points to `Patient/123` ÔÇö the
`Patient` is not copied, it is referenced. If a client needs the complete
patient data, it issues `GET /Patient/123`.

### Reference Types and Resolution {#sec-09en-reference-types}

`FHIR` defines four ways to reference a resource, with distinct implications
for portability and network dependency [@HL7_FHIR_R4]:

**Absolute reference** ÔÇö includes the full server `URL`. Portable across
servers, but requires the target server to be accessible at resolution time:

```json
"subject": { "reference": "https://fhir.ulstmad.pt/Patient/123" }
```

**Relative reference** ÔÇö the most common. The `URL` is relative to the
base server of the transaction. Simple and efficient, but only works within
the context of the same `FHIR` server:

```json
"subject": { "reference": "Patient/123" }
```

**Contained resource** ÔÇö the referenced resource is embedded within the parent
resource, with no `URL` of its own. The `#` prefix identifies the reference
as internal to the current resource:

```json
"subject": { "reference": "#patient-inline" }
```

**Logical reference** ÔÇö carries only the clinical identifier (*business
identifier*), without pointing to any server. Resolution is left to an
external system (e.g. a *Master Patient Index*):

```json
"subject": {
  "identifier": {
    "system": "http://rnsnp.min-saude.pt",
    "value":  "123456789"
  }
}
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Note** ÔÇö Relative references are the default choice in intra-hospital
environments where all resources reside on the same server. Absolute references
are necessary in inter-hospital scenarios. Logical references are the solution
when there is no guarantee that the target server is accessible ÔÇö the clinical
identifier travels; resolution comes later.
:::

## `Bundle` ÔÇö Types and Uses {#sec-09en-bundle-types}

The `Bundle` is the `FHIR` standardised envelope. It allows a set of resources
to be transported as a coherent unit. The five `Bundle` types have distinct
semantics:

| Type | Use | Example |
|:-----|:----|:--------|
| `document` | Immutable clinical document, with date and signature | Discharge summary, episode report |
| `transaction` | Set of atomic operations ÔÇö all or nothing | Create `Patient` + `Encounter` simultaneously |
| `searchset` | Result of a `GET` search | `GET /Patient?family=Silva` ÔåÆ `Bundle` with results |
| `batch` | Set of independent requests ÔÇö without atomicity | Update multiple resources in batch |
| `collection` | Arbitrary grouping of resources | Patient data export |

: FHIR R4 Bundle types and their uses [@HL7_FHIR_R4]. {#tbl-09en-bundle-types}

The most important distinction for interoperability scenarios is between
`transaction` and `batch`. In a `Bundle transaction`, either all operations
succeed or none is persisted ÔÇö an atomic guarantee essential when data
consistency cannot be partial. In a `Bundle batch`, each request is processed
independently: one may fail without affecting the rest.

The `document` type is structurally different from the others: the first
`entry` element must be a `Composition` (the document header resource, with
date, author and sections), and the resulting `Bundle` is considered immutable
by convention ÔÇö it represents a deliberate, signed snapshot of the clinical
state at a given moment.

## Clinical Workflow Resources {#sec-09en-recursos-fluxo}

The resources covered in this chapter form the backbone of any clinical
episode: `Patient` (who is treated), `Practitioner`/`PractitionerRole` (who
treats and in what role), `Organization` (where), `Encounter` (the episode
that articulates them all), `DiagnosticReport` and `Observation` (the results),
and `ImagingStudy` (the bridge to the `PACS`).

### `Patient` ÔÇö the Subject of All Care {#sec-09en-patient}

`Patient` is the most referenced `FHIR` resource. All other clinical resources
ÔÇö `Encounter`, `Observation`, `DiagnosticReport`, `MedicationRequest` ÔÇö point
to it. The fields most relevant to interoperability:

| Field | Meaning | Note |
|:------|:--------|:-----|
| `identifier` | Business identifier ÔÇö `NHC`, `NIF`, `MPI`, health card | A patient may have several, one per system |
| `name` | Structured name: `family` + `given` | `HumanName` allows multiple names and periods |
| `birthDate` | Date of birth (`YYYY-MM-DD`) | Used to disambiguate identity |
| `gender` | `male` \| `female` \| `other` \| `unknown` | Administrative sex ÔÇö not clinical |
| `managingOrganization` | `Reference(Organization)` ÔÇö the managing institution | Each system has its "own" `Patient` |
| `generalPractitioner` | `Reference(Practitioner)` ÔÇö general practitioner | May be an inter-institutional reference |

: Main fields of the FHIR R4 Patient resource [@HL7_FHIR_R4]. {#tbl-09en-patient-fields}

The `identifier` field is the key to interoperability: the same patient exists
in multiple systems with different system `id`s, but the national health
number or the `NIF` is the same in all. Interoperability begins by correctly
identifying the patient in each system.

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Patient",
  "id":           "p-123",
  "identifier": [
    { "system": "http://rnsnp.min-saude.pt",    "value": "999000111"       },
    { "system": "http://ulstmad.pt/processos", "value": "PROC-2024-04512" }
  ],
  "name":        [{ "family": "Ferreira", "given": ["Ana"] }],
  "birthDate":   "1978-04-12",
  "gender":      "female",
  "managingOrganization": { "reference": "Organization/org-ulstmad" }
}
```

### `Practitioner` and `PractitionerRole` ÔÇö Identity and Role {#sec-09en-practitioner}

`FHIR` deliberately separates the **identity** of a healthcare professional
from the **role** they perform in a given institutional context. This
separation is necessary because the same professional may hold different roles
in different organisations: a cardiologist at the Unidade Local de Sa├║de de
Tr├ís-os-Montes e Alto Douro (ULSTMAD) and a consultant at a private hospital,
for example.

**`Practitioner` ÔÇö who they are:**

| Field | Content |
|:------|:--------|
| `identifier` | Professional registration number, `NIF` |
| `name` | Full structured name |
| `telecom` | Professional contact |
| `qualification` | Specialty and certifications |

**`PractitionerRole` ÔÇö where and in what role:**

| Field | Content |
|:------|:--------|
| `practitioner` | `Reference(Practitioner)` ÔÇö who they are |
| `organization` | `Reference(Organization)` ÔÇö where they work |
| `code` | Role: physician, nurse, pharmacistÔÇª |
| `specialty` | Specialty: cardiology, radiologyÔÇª |

```json
/* Practitioner ÔÇö the permanent identity */
{
  "resourceType": "Practitioner",
  "id":           "pr-luiscosta",
  "identifier": [{ "system": "http://ordemmedicos.pt", "value": "C-45231" }],
  "name":        [{ "family": "Costa", "given": ["Lu├¡s"] }]
}
```

```json
/* PractitionerRole ÔÇö the role at ULSTMAD */
{
  "resourceType":  "PractitionerRole",
  "id":            "role-luiscosta-ulstmad",
  "practitioner":  { "reference": "Practitioner/pr-luiscosta" },
  "organization":  { "reference": "Organization/org-ulstmad" },
  "code":      [{ "coding": [{ "code": "doctor" }] }],
  "specialty": [{
    "coding": [{
      "system":  "http://snomed.info/sct",
      "code":    "394579002",
      "display": "Cardiology"
    }]
  }]
}
```

Separating identity from role allows the same professional to appear with
different roles in different contexts without duplicating their personal data
and credentials. Dr. Lu├¡s Costa is a Cardiologist (`specialty`) at ULSTMAD
(`organization`) ÔÇö `PractitionerRole` links all three.

### `Organization` ÔÇö the Institution as a Resource {#sec-09en-organization}

`Organization` represents any formal healthcare entity: hospital, department,
pharmacy, insurer. The `partOf` field allows hierarchies to be represented ÔÇö
a cardiology department is `partOf` the hospital that houses it:

| Field | Meaning | Example |
|:------|:--------|:--------|
| `identifier` | `NIF`, ACSS code, institution `OID` | `urn:oid:2.16.840.1.113883.2.4` |
| `name` | Official organisation name | `Unidade Local de Sa├║de de Tr├ís-os-Montes e Alto Douro` |
| `type` | `prov` (provider) ┬À `dept` (department) ┬À `team` ┬À `govt` ┬À `ins` | `prov` ÔÇö ULSTMAD ┬À `dept` ÔÇö Cardiology Dept |
| `partOf` | `Reference(Organization)` ÔÇö hierarchy | Cardiology Department ÔåÆ ULSTMAD |
| `endpoint` | `URL` of the organisation's `FHIR` server | `https://fhir.ulstmad.pt` |

: Main fields of the FHIR R4 Organization resource [@HL7_FHIR_R4]. {#tbl-09en-organization-fields}

`Organization` is referenced by `Patient.managingOrganization`,
`Encounter.serviceProvider` and `PractitionerRole.organization` ÔÇö it is the
institutional anchor field of almost all other clinical resources.

### `Encounter` ÔÇö the Clinical Episode as a Linking Node {#sec-09en-encounter}

`Encounter` represents a contact between the patient and the healthcare system
ÔÇö a consultation, an inpatient admission, an emergency visit, a teleconsultation.
It is the resource that articulates all others in a clinical episode.

| Field | Values | Role |
|:------|:-------|:-----|
| `status` | `planned` \| `arrived` \| `in-progress` \| `finished` \| `cancelled` | Episode state |
| `class` | `inpatient` \| `outpatient` \| `emergency` \| `virtual` | Contact type |
| `subject` | `Reference(Patient)` | Who is treated |
| `participant` | `Reference(Practitioner)` | Who provides care |
| `serviceProvider` | `Reference(Organization)` | Where it occurs |
| `period` | `start` + `end` | Start and end date/time |
| `reasonCode` | `CodeableConcept` | Reason for visit/admission |

: Main fields of the FHIR R4 Encounter resource [@HL7_FHIR_R4]. {#tbl-09en-encounter-fields}

`Encounter` is the **central node** of the clinical graph: it articulates who
(`Patient`), who treats (`Practitioner`), where (`Organization`) and when
(`period`). All clinical results of an episode ÔÇö `Observation`,
`DiagnosticReport`, `MedicationRequest` ÔÇö reference the `Encounter` to place
the information in its temporal and institutional context.

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Encounter",
  "id":           "enc-2025-04152",
  "status":       "finished",
  "class":        { "code": "outpatient" },
  "subject":         { "reference": "Patient/p-123" },
  "participant": [{ "individual": { "reference": "Practitioner/pr-luiscosta" } }],
  "serviceProvider": { "reference": "Organization/org-ulstmad" },
  "period": {
    "start": "2025-04-15T09:00:00+01:00",
    "end":   "2025-04-15T09:45:00+01:00"
  },
  "reasonCode": [{
    "coding": [{
      "system":  "http://snomed.info/sct",
      "code":    "44054006",
      "display": "Type 2 diabetes mellitus"
    }]
  }]
}
```

```{=html}
<!--
  Diagram: Encounter as central node linking Patient, Practitioner,
  Organization, DiagnosticReport, Observation, ImagingStudy.
  Blue palette (IM).
-->
<div style="overflow-x:auto; margin:1.5rem 0;">
<svg viewBox="0 0 700 320" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="width:100%;max-width:700px;font-family:sans-serif;">
  <rect width="700" height="320" rx="10" fill="#f5f8fc"/>

  <!-- Encounter (centre) -->
  <rect x="265" y="118" width="170" height="52" rx="8" fill="#1a3a6b"/>
  <text x="350" y="139" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Encounter</text>
  <text x="350" y="158" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9"
        fill="#b8d0f0">clinical episode</text>

  <!-- Patient (left) -->
  <rect x="30" y="120" width="140" height="46" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="100" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Patient</text>
  <text x="100" y="156" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">subject</text>
  <line x1="170" y1="144" x2="265" y2="144" stroke="#2d6abf" stroke-width="1.5"
        marker-end="url(#ar1)"/>

  <!-- Organization (top) -->
  <rect x="255" y="26" width="190" height="46" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="350" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Organization</text>
  <text x="350" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">serviceProvider</text>
  <line x1="350" y1="72" x2="350" y2="118" stroke="#2d6abf" stroke-width="1.5"
        marker-end="url(#ar1)"/>

  <!-- Practitioner (right) -->
  <rect x="530" y="120" width="150" height="46" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="605" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Practitioner</text>
  <text x="605" y="156" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">participant</text>
  <line x1="530" y1="144" x2="435" y2="144" stroke="#2d6abf" stroke-width="1.5"
        marker-end="url(#ar1)"/>

  <!-- DiagnosticReport (bottom left) -->
  <rect x="30" y="236" width="170" height="50" rx="6" fill="#4a86d8"/>
  <text x="115" y="256" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">DiagnosticReport</text>
  <text x="115" y="274" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">ÔåÆ Reference(Encounter)</text>
  <line x1="265" y1="165" x2="160" y2="236" stroke="#4a86d8" stroke-width="1.5"
        stroke-dasharray="4" marker-end="url(#ar2)"/>

  <!-- Observation (bottom centre) -->
  <rect x="255" y="236" width="190" height="50" rx="6" fill="#4a86d8"/>
  <text x="350" y="256" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Observation</text>
  <text x="350" y="274" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">ÔåÆ Reference(Encounter)</text>
  <line x1="350" y1="170" x2="350" y2="236" stroke="#4a86d8" stroke-width="1.5"
        stroke-dasharray="4" marker-end="url(#ar2)"/>

  <!-- ImagingStudy (bottom right) -->
  <rect x="500" y="236" width="170" height="50" rx="6" fill="#4a86d8"/>
  <text x="585" y="256" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">ImagingStudy</text>
  <text x="585" y="274" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">ÔåÆ Reference(Encounter)</text>
  <line x1="435" y1="165" x2="540" y2="236" stroke="#4a86d8" stroke-width="1.5"
        stroke-dasharray="4" marker-end="url(#ar2)"/>

  <text x="350" y="313" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">
    Figure 9.1 ÔÇö Encounter as the central node of the FHIR clinical graph
  </text>

  <defs>
    <marker id="ar1" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7"
            refX="6" refY="3.5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L7,3.5 L0,7 Z" fill="#2d6abf"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="ar2" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7"
            refX="6" refY="3.5" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L7,3.5 L0,7 Z" fill="#4a86d8"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>
</svg>
</div>
```

### `DiagnosticReport` and `Observation` ÔÇö Structured Clinical Result {#sec-09en-diagnostic}

`DiagnosticReport` aggregates individual `Observation`s. A laboratory report
ÔÇö a full blood count, for example ÔÇö is a `DiagnosticReport` with a list of
`Observation`s, one per measured value.

**`DiagnosticReport` ÔÇö the report:**

| Field | Content |
|:------|:--------|
| `code` | Exam type (`LOINC`) ÔÇö e.g. `"Full blood count"` |
| `status` | `registered` \| `partial` \| `final` \| `amended` \| `entered-in-error` |
| `subject` | `Reference(Patient)` |
| `performer` | `Reference(Practitioner \| Organization)` |
| `result` | List of `Reference(Observation)` |
| `conclusion` | Narrative conclusion text |

**`Observation` ÔÇö the individual measurement:**

| Field | Content |
|:------|:--------|
| `code` | What was measured (`LOINC`) ÔÇö e.g. `"Haemoglobin"` |
| `value[x]` | The value: `valueQuantity`, `valueString`, `valueCodeableConcept` |
| `status` | `final` \| `preliminary` \| `amended` \| `cancelled` |
| `subject` | `Reference(Patient)` |
| `encounter` | `Reference(Encounter)` ÔÇö clinical context |
| `referenceRange` | Reference range for interpretation |

The `value[x]` field uses the `[x]` notation to indicate polymorphism: the
value type may be `valueQuantity` (e.g. `6.4 mmol/L`), `valueString` (e.g.
a textual description), or `valueCodeableConcept` (e.g. `"positive"` coded in
`SNOMED`). Only one type may be present in a given resource.

```json
{
  "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
  "id":           "dr-fbc-2025",
  "status":       "final",
  "code": {
    "coding": [{
      "system":  "http://loinc.org",
      "code":    "58410-2",
      "display": "CBC panel - Blood by Automated count"
    }],
    "text": "Full blood count"
  },
  "subject":   { "reference": "Patient/p-123" },
  "encounter": { "reference": "Encounter/enc-2025-04152" },
  "performer": [{ "reference": "Organization/lab-ulstmad" }],
  "result": [
    { "reference": "Observation/obs-hgb-001" },
    { "reference": "Observation/obs-plt-001" }
  ],
  "conclusion": "Full blood count within normal parameters."
}
```

```json
{
  "resourceType":   "Observation",
  "id":             "obs-hgb-001",
  "status":         "final",
  "code": {
    "coding": [{
      "system":  "http://loinc.org",
      "code":    "718-7",
      "display": "Hemoglobin [Mass/volume] in Blood"
    }],
    "text": "Haemoglobin"
  },
  "subject":   { "reference": "Patient/p-123" },
  "encounter": { "reference": "Encounter/enc-2025-04152" },
  "valueQuantity": {
    "value": 13.8, "unit": "g/dL",
    "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org"
  },
  "referenceRange": [{
    "low":  { "value": 12, "unit": "g/dL" },
    "high": { "value": 16, "unit": "g/dL" }
  }]
}
```

The `DiagnosticReport.result` field is a list of references ÔÇö each
`Observation` can be accessed and interpreted individually. This granularity
allows a clinical alerting system to access the haemoglobin `Observation`
directly without downloading the complete report.

### `ImagingStudy` ÔÇö the `DICOM`Ôåö`FHIR` Bridge {#sec-09en-imagingstudy}

`ImagingStudy` represents a `DICOM` study in the `FHIR` model without
duplicating the images ÔÇö these remain in the `PACS`. The resource acts as
a pointer: it contains the study metadata and a reference to the `WADO-RS`
server where the images can be accessed.

| `DICOM` Concept | `FHIR` Field | Notes |
|:----------------|:------------|:------|
| Study UID (0020,000D) | `identifier` (`system="urn:dicom:uid"`) | Unique and permanent reference to the study |
| Patient ID (0010,0020) | `subject ÔåÆ Reference(Patient)` | Identity resolution required ÔÇö not direct mapping |
| Modality (0008,0060) | `series.modality` (`CT`, `MR`, `US`ÔÇª) | Each series has its own modality |
| Series UID (0020,000E) | `series.uid` | A series = set of `DICOM` instances |
| SOP Instance UID (0008,0018) | `series.instance.uid` | An instance = an individual `DICOM` image |
| `WADO-RS` URL | `ImagingStudy.endpoint ÔåÆ Endpoint.address` | Standard protocol for accessing `DICOM` images via `HTTP` |

: Mapping between DICOM concepts and FHIR fields in ImagingStudy [@HL7_FHIR_R4; @DICOM_PS318]. {#tbl-09en-imagingstudy}

The `subject` field requires particular attention: the `DICOM` Patient ID does
not map directly to the system `id` of a `FHIR` `Patient` resource. The two
systems use independent identification schemes; identity resolution ÔÇö the
process of determining which `FHIR` `Patient` corresponds to a given `DICOM`
Patient ID ÔÇö is performed using matching algorithms based on `NIF`, name and
date of birth, or by an `MPI`.

::: {.callout-note}
**Note** ÔÇö `WADO-RS` (*Web Access to DICOM Objects ÔÇö RESTful Services*) is
the `DICOMweb` protocol that exposes `DICOM` studies via an `HTTP` `API`. A
`GET` request to the `WADO-RS` `URL` of a study returns the images in `DICOM`
or `JPEG` format. `ImagingStudy` in `FHIR` stores this `URL` in the `endpoint`
field, allowing any `FHIR` application to access `PACS` images without
needing to know the details of the legacy `DICOM` protocol.
:::

## Distributed Interoperability {#sec-09en-interop}

### Scenario 1 ÔÇö Referencing Across Institutions {#sec-09en-interop-cross}

In this scenario, Hospital A is the Unidade Local de Sa├║de de Tr├ís-os-Montes e
Alto Douro (ULSTMAD) and Hospital B is the Centro Hospitalar Universit├írio de
S├úo Jo├úo (CHUSJ).

Hospital B (CHUSJ) creates a `DiagnosticReport` for a patient registered at
Hospital A (ULSTMAD). The `Patient` data is not copied ÔÇö it is referenced by
the absolute `URL` of Hospital A's server:

```json
{
  "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",
  "id":           "dr-chusj-001",
  "status":       "final",
  "subject": {
    "reference": "https://fhir.ulstmad.pt/Patient/123",
    "display":   "Ana Ferreira"
  },
  "code":      { "text": "Chest CT" },
  "performer": [{ "reference": "Organization/org-chusj" }]
}
```

When Hospital B needs the complete patient data, it resolves the reference
with a `GET`:

```
GET https://fhir.ulstmad.pt/Patient/123
ÔåÆ Hospital A responds with the complete Patient resource
```

This architecture has a real limitation: if Hospital A's server is not
accessible at the time of the request, the absolute reference cannot be
resolved. `FHIR` does not guarantee availability ÔÇö it is the responsibility
of the institutions to ensure SLA agreements and high-availability
infrastructure.

### Scenario 2 ÔÇö Transfer and Continuity of Care {#sec-09en-interop-transfer}

Hospital A will transfer a patient to Hospital B. The flow uses a `Bundle` of
type `document`.

**Step 1 ÔÇö Hospital A creates the `Bundle(document)`:**

It includes a `Composition` (mandatory header ÔÇö date, author, sections), the
`Patient`, the discharge `Encounter`, the `DiagnosticReport`, and the
`MedicationRequest`. The `Bundle document` is immutable by convention ÔÇö it
represents a deliberate, signed snapshot of the clinical state at the moment
of discharge.

**Step 2 ÔÇö Transmission via authenticated `POST`:**

```
POST https://fhir.chusj.pt/Bundle
Authorization: Bearer <token OAuth2>
```

The `POST` is authenticated via `OAuth2`/`SMART on FHIR` ÔÇö required in
production by the `GDPR` (Regulation (EU) 2016/679, Art. 32). The `Bundle`
is stored at both institutions: one copy at Hospital A, another at Hospital B.

**Step 3 ÔÇö Hospital B receives and processes:**

Hospital B stores the `Bundle` and resolves the patient identity using the
shared identifier (`NIF`):

- If the patient already existed locally ÔåÆ associates without duplication.
- If new ÔåÆ creates a local `Patient` with the `NIF` as anchor, and retains
  the absolute reference to Hospital A as an additional identifier.

**Step 4 ÔÇö Continuity established:**

The physician at Hospital B consults the `DiagnosticReport` and the
`MedicationRequest` from Hospital A without any manual intervention. `FHIR`
works like the web: each resource has its `URL`, and other systems reference
it by `URL`. The `Bundle(document)` is the equivalent of *saving the page to
read offline* ÔÇö a deliberate snapshot for when the connection cannot be
relied upon.

## Reference Resolution ÔÇö Comparison {#sec-09en-resolucao}

```{=html}
<!--
  Diagram: four FHIR reference types with pros/cons.
  Blue palette (IM).
-->
<div style="overflow-x:auto; margin:1.5rem 0;">
<svg viewBox="0 0 700 230" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     style="width:100%;max-width:700px;font-family:sans-serif;">
  <rect width="700" height="230" rx="10" fill="#f5f8fc"/>
  <text x="350" y="22" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">FHIR reference types ÔÇö comparison</text>

  <!-- Relative -->
  <rect x="20" y="34" width="155" height="164" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="97" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Relative</text>
  <text x="97" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">"Patient/123"</text>
  <rect x="30" y="80" width="135" height="108" rx="4" fill="#1a3a6b"/>
  <text x="97" y="96"  text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Simple</text>
  <text x="97" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Efficient</text>
  <text x="97" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù Local to server</text>
  <text x="97" y="144" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù Not portable</text>
  <text x="97" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">Use: intra-hospital</text>
  <text x="97" y="178" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff"> </text>

  <!-- Absolute -->
  <rect x="185" y="34" width="155" height="164" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="262" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Absolute</text>
  <text x="262" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">"https://fhir.h.pt/..."</text>
  <rect x="195" y="80" width="135" height="108" rx="4" fill="#1a3a6b"/>
  <text x="262" y="96"  text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Portable</text>
  <text x="262" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Inter-hospital</text>
  <text x="262" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù Requires network</text>
  <text x="262" y="144" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù SLA needed</text>
  <text x="262" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">Use: inter-hospital</text>
  <text x="262" y="178" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff"> </text>

  <!-- Contained -->
  <rect x="350" y="34" width="155" height="164" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="427" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Contained</text>
  <text x="427" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">"#patient-inline"</text>
  <rect x="360" y="80" width="135" height="108" rx="4" fill="#1a3a6b"/>
  <text x="427" y="96"  text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Self-sufficient</text>
  <text x="427" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô No network needed</text>
  <text x="427" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù No identity</text>
  <text x="427" y="144" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù Not reusable</text>
  <text x="427" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">Use: no server</text>
  <text x="427" y="178" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">available</text>

  <!-- Logical -->
  <rect x="515" y="34" width="165" height="164" rx="6" fill="#2d6abf"/>
  <text x="597" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10"
        font-weight="bold" fill="#fff">Logical</text>
  <text x="597" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8"
        fill="#d0e4ff">{ identifier: ... }</text>
  <rect x="525" y="80" width="145" height="108" rx="4" fill="#1a3a6b"/>
  <text x="597" y="96"  text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô Portable</text>
  <text x="597" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#90b8e8">Ô£ô No URL needed</text>
  <text x="597" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù External resolution</text>
  <text x="597" y="144" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#fca5a5">Ô£ù Requires MPI</text>
  <text x="597" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">Use: clinical ID</text>
  <text x="597" y="178" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#d0e4ff">as anchor</text>

  <text x="350" y="218" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" fill="#64748b">
    Figure 9.2 ÔÇö Comparison of the four FHIR reference types
  </text>
</svg>
</div>
```

## Real Limitations of `FHIR` Interoperability {#sec-09en-limitations}

Technical interoperability is a necessary condition ÔÇö but not sufficient.
The real challenges of `FHIR` interoperability in production are primarily
semantic and organisational.

### Patient Identity {#sec-09en-limitations-identity}

The same patient has different `ID`s at each institution. Without an `MPI`
(*Master Patient Index*) or a matching algorithm, cross-references cannot be
safely resolved. In Portugal, the national health number (`SNS`) serves as
the national identifier ÔÇö but not all systems record it consistently. The
`NIF` is an alternative, but raises privacy concerns.

### Resource Versioning {#sec-09en-limitations-versioning}

A reference points to the current version of the resource ÔÇö but data changes.
The `meta.versionId` field and the `_history` endpoint allow access to
previous versions:

```
GET /Patient/p-123/_history/2    ÔåÉ specific version
GET /Patient/p-123/_history      ÔåÉ complete history
```

These mechanisms require the `FHIR` server to implement them ÔÇö and many
production servers do not do so completely.

### Terminology Conflicts {#sec-09en-limitations-terminology}

Two systems may use different terminologies for the same clinical concept.
`FHIR` recommends ÔÇö but does not mandate ÔÇö the use of `SNOMED CT`, `LOINC`
and `ICD` as standard terminologies. The `ConceptMap` resource allows explicit
mappings to be defined between two code systems, but keeping those mappings
up to date requires continuous organisational effort.

### Authorisation and Privacy {#sec-09en-limitations-authorisation}

Accessing a resource on another server requires authentication and
authorisation. `SMART on FHIR` defines the `OAuth2` flow for clinical
applications ÔÇö the mechanism that allows an application to request
authorisation from a `FHIR` server to access a patient's data, with the
patient's explicit consent. `IHE ATNA` (*Audit Trail and Node Authentication*)
defines how access to clinical data is logged and audited. Without these
mechanisms, sharing clinical data between institutions violates the `GDPR`.

The complete interoperability equation can be summarised as:

| Dimension | Solution |
|:----------|:---------|
| Format | `FHIR` R4 |
| Semantics | `SNOMED CT` ┬À `LOINC` ┬À `ICD` ┬À `ConceptMap` |
| Identity | `MPI` ┬À `NIF` ┬À national health number |
| Security | `SMART on FHIR` ┬À `IHE ATNA` ┬À `GDPR` |

: The four dimensions of healthcare interoperability. {#tbl-09en-interop-dims}

## Exercises {#sec-09en-exercicios .unnumbered}

**1.** A private laboratory receives a blood sample with the patient's `NIF`
but without access to the hospital's `FHIR` server where the patient is
registered.

(a) Which `FHIR` mechanism allows the laboratory to include minimal patient
    information in the result without needing a resolvable reference?
(b) Which reference type does the laboratory use in the `subject` field of
    the `Observation`?
(c) When the result reaches the hospital server, how does the hospital
    associate the `Observation` with the correct `Patient`?

**2.** Analyse the following `Encounter` fragment:

```json
{
  "resourceType": "Encounter",
  "id":     "enc-emergency-001",
  "status": "in-progress",
  "class":  { "code": "emergency" },
  "subject":         { "reference": "Patient/p-789" },
  "participant": [{ "individual": { "reference": "Practitioner/pr-234" } }],
  "serviceProvider": { "reference": "Organization/org-hgv" },
  "period":          { "start": "2025-11-03T02:15:00+00:00" }
}
```

(a) What state is the episode in? What type of contact does it represent?
(b) The `period` field has no `end`. What does this mean clinically?
(c) What would be the `URL` to retrieve the `Practitioner` treating the patient?
(d) If a blood pressure `Observation` is recorded during this episode, what
    reference must it have in the `encounter` field?

**3.** Explain the difference between `Practitioner` and `PractitionerRole`
and justify why `FHIR` separates the two concepts into distinct resources.
Give an example with a professional working in two different contexts.

**4.** A Portuguese hospital prepares a `Bundle` of type `document` to send
a patient's clinical record to a private clinic.

(a) Which resource must be the first `entry` in the `Bundle document`? What
    is its role?
(b) The `Bundle` is `POST`ed without authentication. Which European regulation
    is potentially violated?
(c) After receiving the `Bundle`, the clinic finds that the patient already
    exists locally with a different `id`. Which `Patient` field should be used
    to confirm they are the same person?
(d) Which property of the `Bundle document` ensures that the received data
    cannot be altered retroactively?

**5.** Consider two systems communicating via `FHIR`. System A uses
`SNOMED CT` to code diagnoses; System B uses `ICD-10-CM`.

(a) Which `FHIR` resource type allows the mapping between the two code systems
    to be defined?
(b) The `code` field of a `Condition` has a `required` binding to an
    `ICD-10` `ValueSet`. System A attempts to send a diagnosis coded only in
    `SNOMED CT`. What happens during validation?
(c) Which `FHIR` structure would allow the diagnosis to be carried in both
    terminologies simultaneously?

**6.** A reference in an `Encounter` points to
`"https://fhir.ulstmad.pt/Patient/p-123"`. The ULSTMAD server is temporarily
unavailable.

(a) What type of reference is this?
(b) What impact does the server's unavailability have on the ability to
    process the `Encounter`?
(c) Which alternative reference type could make the `Encounter` processable
    even without a connection to ULSTMAD? What would its limitations be?
